title: Crepidium mambulilingense
taxonomy: Crepidium mambulilingense is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is also classified as being part of the genus Crepidium. Within the genus, it is further classified as a member of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. As a result, its full classification is Crepidium subtribe Pleurothallidinae, family Orchidaceae.

intro: Crepidium mambulilingense is a species of orchid native to the Philippines, characterized by its unique, terrestrial habit and attractive flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium mambulilingense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Crepidium mambulilingense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
